name: Ascent CFO Solutions
description_short: Ascent CFO Solutions provides fractional and interim CFO services, accounting, and financial advisory to small and mid-sized businesses seeking expert financial leadership without full-time executive costs.
description_long:
Ascent CFO Solutions is a Denver and Boulder-based financial services firm specializing in fractional CFO services for small to mid-sized businesses. The company was recognized on the Inc. 5000 List of America's Fastest Growing Private Companies, indicating significant growth in the fractional finance executive market. They position themselves as a scalable alternative to hiring a full-time Chief Financial Officer, offering experienced financial leadership that adapts to different business stages and needs.

The company offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including fractional CFO support, interim CFO services during transitions, fractional accounting, data analytics and real-time financial dashboards, virtual CFO services delivered remotely, M&A advisory (buy-side and sell-side), capital fundraising support with financial modeling, and cash flow forecasting with scenario planning. Their service model is designed to be flexible, allowing businesses to engage part-time CFO support or full-time interim leadership depending on organizational needs. They serve diverse industries including SaaS and technology, professional services, construction and real estate, manufacturing, retail and ecommerce, healthcare, and financial services.

According to their website, Ascent differentiates itself through a full-stack team with specialized knowledge across industries and growth stages, integration as part of the leadership team rather than external consultants, proven track record guiding companies from pre-revenue through acquisition, and flexible engagement models. Their CFOs are described as being in the "prime of their career" with deep experience across growth stages. The website emphasizes becoming an "invested stakeholder" in client success and building enduring capabilities beyond the engagement period.

However, potential clients should note that this is a service-based advisory firm rather than a direct lender or funding source. The website does not clearly disclose pricing structures, making cost comparison difficult for prospective clients. Client testimonials are limited to three quotes on the homepage, and specific case studies are referenced but not detailed in the available content. The firm appears focused on growth-stage and established companies rather than early-stage bootstrapped startups, based on their service emphasis and client profiles mentioned.
pros: Full-stack financial team with specialized expertise across multiple industries (SaaS, construction, healthcare, professional services, manufacturing, retail)
Flexible engagement model offering both fractional part-time and full-time interim CFO support that scales with business growth
Comprehensive service offering including fractional CFO, accounting, M&A advisory, capital fundraising, and data analytics with real-time dashboards
Proven track record with Inc. 5000 recognition and documented client success (testimonial mentions guidance from Techstars through Atlassian acquisition)
Remote/virtual service delivery available nationwide, not limited to Colorado locations
Experienced CFOs in prime of career rather than junior staff or retired consultants
Emphasis on integration as true leadership team members with long-term, proactive partnership approach
cons: Pricing structure not disclosed on website, making cost comparison impossible before consultation
Limited client case study details provided; only three short testimonials visible on homepage
Appears primarily focused on growth-stage and established companies rather than early-stage bootstrapped startups
No transparent information about typical engagement length, minimum contract terms, or service minimums
As advisory services firm, they do not provide actual funding or capital—only guidance on raising it
best_for: Growth-stage SaaS and technology companies needing financial expertise during rapid scaling or fundraising rounds
Small to mid-sized professional services, construction, or manufacturing firms seeking experienced CFO-level financial strategy without full-time executive salary
Businesses in transition phases requiring interim CFO leadership during ownership changes or critical restructuring periods
Companies preparing for M&A activity (acquisition or sale) needing buy-side or sell-side financial advisory and due diligence support
diagnosis: Ascent CFO Solutions is best for established or growth-stage small-to-mid-sized businesses that need experienced CFO-level financial strategy and leadership but cannot justify or afford a full-time Chief Financial Officer salary. The main caveat is that they are an advisory and services firm, not a funding source—clients seeking actual business loans or capital should look elsewhere, though Ascent can advise on fundraising strategy.
services: Fractional CFO services (part-time strategic financial leadership)
Interim CFO services (full-time leadership during transitions)
Data analytics and real-time financial dashboards
Capital fundraising and investor-ready financial modeling
Cash flow forecasting with rolling projections and scenario planning
Financial process setup and systems establishment
Strategic financial insights and forward-looking forecasts
Industry-specific financial expertise across SaaS, professional services, construction, healthcare, and others
